Event: Milhouse sleeps on Lisa's bedroom floor to be close to her
Explanation: He humiliates and endangers himself while creeping Lisa out, gaining no real benefit.

Event: Milhouse tries to hide his crush on Lisa despite being terrible at it
Explanation: His secrecy backfires socially on him while having little effect on Lisa beyond mild awkwardness.

Event: Milhouse openly expresses jealousy when Bart spends much time with Nelson
Explanation: Jealous outbursts strain his bond with Bart without helping him.

Event: Milhouse jumps over a waterfall exclaiming he can't live without Bart
Explanation: He nearly kills himself for Bart, providing no clear help to anyone.

Event: Milhouse runs a Kidz Newz segment showing how to dispose of a soiled mattress
Explanation: He likely gains small status and gives viewers practical, if embarrassing, information.

Event: Milhouse claims a burglar wet his bed and his Capital City friends stop liking him
Explanation: His explanation leaves him ostracized, helping no one else either.

Event: Bart takes Milhouse's inhaler to use as a snorkel
Explanation: Bart benefits from a makeshift snorkel while seriously endangering Milhouse’s health.

Event: Milhouse rolls a grapefruit that gets stuck under the bus brake pedal
Explanation: His careless act crashes the bus and strands everyone with no gain to himself.

Event: Milhouse tries to encourage reformed Bart to send Willy off a cliff
Explanation: He urges gratuitous harm to Willy without any benefit to himself or Bart.

Event: Milhouse frequently runs away when things start to smell bad
Explanation: He protects his own comfort and health while causing at most minor inconvenience to others.

Event: Milhouse finally stands up to Bart and ends their friendship
Explanation: He protects his own dignity and boundaries even though it hurts Bart emotionally.

Event: Milhouse persists in pursuing Lisa believing niceness will win her over
Explanation: His one-sided efforts waste his energy and make Lisa uncomfortable without benefiting her.

Event: Milhouse passes Lisa's note to Nelson, who mistakes it as from Milhouse
Explanation: His attempt to help Lisa results in him being beaten into a coma.

Event: Milhouse accepts inheriting Bart's permanent record (by inaction/doormat behavior)
Explanation: Allowing Bart’s record to stick to him gravely harms his future for no one else’s gain.

Event: Kirk sells the family house to fund Milhouse’s extravagant birthday party
Explanation: They lose a fundamental asset for fleeting celebration, leaving the family worse off.

Event: Milhouse cheats on Lisa shortly after winning her over
Explanation: He pursues short-term gratification while damaging Lisa and their relationship.

Event: Milhouse reveals Bart’s true identity to Bart’s girlfriend, causing breakup
Explanation: Acting from hurt and jealousy, he sabotages Bart’s relationship for petty emotional payoff.

Event: Milhouse tells college students Lisa is only eight out of jealousy
Explanation: He destroys Lisa’s new friendships purely to satisfy his own envy.

Event: Milhouse publicly reveals Lisa’s ancestor failed to protect the escaped slave
Explanation: He humiliates Lisa during her presentation for spite and the satisfaction of being “right.”

Event: Milhouse hinders Bart and Lisa’s efforts to save him on the island in "Das Bus"
Explanation: His panicky behavior worsens everyone’s danger without improving his own chances.

Event: Milhouse uses his Italian around his grandma to avoid being beaten
Explanation: He adapts to her demand for Italian, reducing punishment for himself while pleasing her.

Event: Milhouse behaves with bad-boy stoicism after his parents are presumed dead
Explanation: His composure helps him cope and unexpectedly earns respect and affection from others.

Event: Kirk and Luann overprotect Milhouse and hide Nemo’s mother’s death
Explanation: Their coddling causes trauma and a fireman callout without truly helping Milhouse.

Event: Milhouse hides under the couch after learning Nemo’s mother died
Explanation: His extreme avoidance harms his own functioning and forces others to intervene.

Event: Firemen pry Milhouse out from under the couch
Explanation: Rescuing him restores safety and functions as their duty with little downside.

Event: Bart gives Milhouse a haircut that makes him look like Kirk
Explanation: Bart gains the ability to pass as an adult while leaving Milhouse with an embarrassing look.

Event: Milhouse adopts a bad-boy persona in "Little Orphan Millie" to be cooler
Explanation: The persona raises his social status and makes him more accepted by others.
